chunk_summary: Low-cost Indian meal templates and audit-ready goals for addressing chronic undernutrition in children and adolescents, with practical meal plans, PM POSHAN integration, and follow-up strategies for stunting and wasting management.
learning_objectives: Understand low-cost meal templates for toddlers, school-age children, and adolescents to address chronic undernutrition.
Learn how to integrate PM POSHAN scheme guidelines into nutritional counseling and follow-up.
Develop audit-ready goals for tracking adherence to nutritional interventions.
Recognize the importance of follow-up schedules based on stunting and wasting severity.
key_facts: For toddlers (12–23 months), a feasible meal plan includes porridge/upma with oil/ghee, khichdi with dal and vegetables, and continued breastfeeding if applicable.
School-age children should consume poha/upma with peanuts and milk for breakfast, and access PM POSHAN school meals, which have published calorie and protein norms.
Adolescents, especially girls at risk of anemia, should pair iron-rich foods with vitamin C and prioritize a protein-rich breakfast.
Audit-ready goals include adding one daily protein serving, replacing tea and biscuits with milk and a home snack, and ensuring school meal attendance.
Follow-up schedules: 2–4 weeks for stunting-only cases initially, then every 6–8 weeks; higher frequency for wasting cases.
reference_quote: When school meals are accessed through the PM POSHAN scheme, use the published calorie and protein norms as counseling support, and ask families to report the number of school-meal days per week to make adherence measurable.
document_key_topics: Low-cost Indian meal templates for chronic undernutrition
PM POSHAN scheme integration in nutritional counseling
Follow-up schedules for stunting and wasting management
